topic: Comprehensive Revision of Third Term Topics

subUnits.title: 11.1 Comprehensive Revision: Trading Account, P&L Account, Balance Sheet, Control Accounts & Depreciation
notes: Comprehensive Third Term Revision
Key Concepts:
1. Trading and Profit & Loss Account with Adjustments:
- Gross Profit = Sales - Cost of Goods Sold
- Net Profit = Gross Profit + Other Incomes - Expenses
- Adjustments: Bad debts, provision for bad debts, discounts, prepayments, accruals, depreciation
2. Balance Sheet Preparation:
- Assets = Liabilities + Capital
- Classification: Fixed/Current Assets, Non-Current/Current Liabilities
- Formats: T-shape and vertical methods
3. Control Accounts:
- Sales Ledger Control Account: Summarizes debtors' transactions
- Purchases Ledger Control Account: Summarizes creditors' transactions
- Contra entries, bad debts, discounts affect control accounts
4. Depreciation Methods:
- Straight-Line: Equal amount each year = (Cost - Scrap Value) / Useful Life
- Reducing Balance: Fixed percentage on net book value each year
- Posting: Debit Depreciation Expense, Credit Accumulated Depreciation
5. Common Examination Questions:
- Preparation of Trading, P&L Account with adjustments
- Balance Sheet preparation in vertical format
- Control account preparation and reconciliation
- Depreciation calculation using both methods
6. Revision Strategy:
- Solve past WAEC/NECO questions under timed conditions
- Review formulas, units, and sign conventions
- Practice diagram drawing (account formats, ledger postings)
- Focus on common student errors and marking scheme requirements
Nigerian Context:
- Align with WAEC/NECO practical and theory standards
- Emphasize local applications (credit sales, utility bills, asset depreciation)
- Practice calculation-heavy and concept-based questions
Conclusion:
- Master all third term topics and interconnections
- Practice past questions under exam conditions
- Focus on units, direction, and real-life applications
- Improve problem-solving speed and accuracy for exams

quiz.explanation: Gross Profit = Sales - Cost of Goods Sold in Trading Account.

quiz.q: Provision for bad debts is shown in Balance Sheet as:
quiz.explanation: Provision for bad debts is deducted from debtors to show net realizable value.

quiz.q: In vertical Balance Sheet format, items are listed:
opts: Assets on left, liabilities on right
Assets first, then liabilities and capital
quiz.explanation: Vertical format lists assets first, followed by liabilities and capital below.
Sales Ledger Control Account represents the total of all debtors' (customers') accounts.
Straight-line depreciation = (Cost - Scrap Value) ÷ Useful Life.

quiz.q: In Nigerian context, adjustments in final accounts are essential for:
opts: Tax compliance, audit readiness, and bank loan applications
quiz.explanation: FIRS, auditors, and Nigerian banks require properly adjusted final accounts for compliance and assessment.

quiz.q: A common error in depreciation calculation is:
opts: Ignoring scrap value in reducing balance method
quiz.explanation: Ignoring scrap value or using wrong net book value causes errors in reducing balance depreciation.

quiz.q: To solve control account problems, you should:
opts: Post totals from journals and reconcile with subsidiary ledger
quiz.explanation: Control accounts require posting totals from journals and reconciliation with subsidiary ledger totals.
Prepayments ensure expenses are matched to the period they relate to (matching principle).

quiz.q: When preparing for exams, you should:
opts: Practice past questions with timed conditions
quiz.explanation: Timed past question practice builds familiarity, speed, and exam readiness.
